hero.title: What is Cisco DNA Center?
hero.subtitle: Central management and orchestration platform for modern enterprise networks
hero.description: Cisco DNA Center unifies device inventory, policy, automation, and assurance so network teams can operate with intent-based accuracy and speed.

cards.title: Enterprise
cards.desc: Campus, branch and edge networks in one control plane

cards.title: Automated
cards.desc: Provisioning, policy and assurance with intent based workflows

cards.title: Secure
cards.desc: ISE identity integration and segmentation for every device

workflow.title: Design
workflow.desc: Create topology, segmentation and wireless policies

workflow.title: Provision
workflow.desc: Deploy devices using templates and automation

workflow.title: Assurance
workflow.desc: Monitor health using telemetry and analytics

workflow.title: Security
workflow.desc: Integrate ISE for identity based access

operations.title: Live Operations in One View
operations.desc: Visualize device health, fabric policy and client experience instantly.

sops.title: Pass VRF / Virtual Networks
steps: Prerequisite: Ensure underlay routing and L3 connectivity between DNA Center and fabric devices, and that VRFs are defined on edge/border nodes.
UI: Go to Design > Network Settings > Virtual Networks. Create or map a Virtual Network and associate the required VRF name and routing domain.
Assign: Map subnets and VLANs to the Virtual Network, add path endpoints and ensure proper Segment IDs (VNID/VXLAN) if using overlay.
Deploy: Apply the virtual network to site(s) via the provisioning workflow (Design > Provision). Select edge/border nodes for routing.
Verify: Use Assurance > Network Health and the inventory to confirm devices report the correct VRF/virtual network and that routes are present on border nodes.
Rollback: Remove mapping or revert the provisioning snapshot in DNA Center and validate route withdrawal on edge devices.

sops.title: Configure Policy
steps: UI: Navigate to Policy > Groups > Host/Endpoint Groups. Create Scalable Group Tags (SGTs) or identity groups as required.
Define Policies: Under Policy > Policy Profiles, create rules (source group, destination group, services/ports, action allow/deny).
Apply Intent: Use intent-based templates to map policy to sites, virtual networks, or device groups. Save and commit.
Automation: Optionally use the DNA Center API to push policies programmatically: POST /dna/intent/api/v1/policy (use proper API payload and authentication).
Verify: Use Assurance > Policy Compliance and live flow tracing to confirm enforcement. Run sample access flows from test endpoints.
Rollback: Disable or revert policy from the policy history view, and re-run traces to confirm expected behavior.

sops.title: Download Syslog
steps: Locate Logs: In DNA Center go to Assurance > Events > Logs or Maintenance > Tools > System Logs depending on version.
Filter: Use timeframe, device, severity, and category filters to narrow results (e.g., auth, interface, OSPF, BGP).
Export: Use the export or download icon to save logs as CSV/JSON. Alternatively use the API: GET /dna/intent/api/v1/event with query params.

Relevant Fields: Typical syslog entries include timestamp, device name/IP, severity, facility, process, message. For network forensic purposes capture correlation IDs, interface names, and session identifiers.
Retention & Storage: Ensure backups of exported logs are stored in a secure, versioned archive for audits.

sops.title: Packet Capture
steps: UI Tool: Use Assurance > Troubleshooting > Packet Capture (path differs by version). Select device, interface, capture filters (src/dst IP, ports), and capture duration.
Start Capture: Begin the capture and reproduce the issue (or run test traffic). Stop when sufficient packets are collected.
Download & Analyze: Download the pcap and open in Wireshark. Look for TCP handshake, retransmits, latency, and application-layer issues.
API: Use DNA Center packet-capture APIs where available to trigger captures programmatically and retrieve PCAPs.
Security Note: Mask or sanitize sensitive payloads before sharing externally; keep pcaps in secured storage.

sops.title: 802.1X Authentication
steps: Prerequisites: ISE integration with DNA Center, valid certificates, radius profiles, and switch ports configured for dot1x or MAB fallback.
ISE Setup: Create authentication and authorization policies in ISE (policy sets, identity sources, profiling rules).
DNA Center: Under Platform > Identity Services (or similar), add ISE as a northbound service (pxGrid/REST) and ensure shared secrets and certificates match.
Port Profiles: Create port profiles or templates in DNA Center that enable 802.1X with required VLANs for authenticated guests/devices.
Test Flow: Connect a test client; observe authentication steps in ISE and the endpoint details appearing in DNA Center (identity, SGT assignment).
Troubleshoot: Use live logs in ISE (Authentication logs) and DNA Center assurance traces. Verify RADIUS accounting and EAP method compatibility.

sops.title: SD Access Implementation
steps: Design: Plan underlay IP addressing, seed nodes, border nodes, and virtual network mapping. Document VRFs, VLANs, and SGT strategy.
Prechecks: Validate device compatibility, software image levels, and ensure network connectivity between DNA Center and devices.
Fabric Creation: In DNA Center use Design > SD-Access > Fabric to create a new fabric, select seed nodes, and define control plane parameters.
Underlay & Overlay: Configure underlay routing (OSPF/BGP), then enable overlay (VXLAN) and assign sites to the fabric.
ISE Integration: Ensure SGTs, policy sets, and pxGrid connectivity are configured so ISE can provide identity mapping to DNA Center.
Validation: Use topology and assurance dashboards to verify endpoint registration, SGT propagation, and path traces across fabric nodes.

sops.title: Provision Switch
steps: Prechecks: Ensure device is reachable (management IP), proper credentials exist in DNA Center, and device is supported.
Discovery: Go to Provision > Discovery, add IP ranges or use Plug-and-Play to discover the device.
Inventory: Confirm device appears in Inventory > Devices. Check model, OS version, and hardware details.
Template Assignment: Create or select a provisioning template (feature templates or CLI templates) that includes interface, VLAN, port-channel, and management settings.
Provision: Use the Provision workflow to assign roles (edge, access), push templates, and apply site mappings. Monitor the provisioning task for success or errors.
Verify: Run device tests (ping, config diff, show run checks) in DNA Center and use Assurance to confirm traffic flows as intended.
